judge_result.judge:
@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
After correctly retrieving 2020 citations and paper documents, the agent’s execute_python step misclassified CHI papers by using a naive extract_venue function that labeled a paper as CHI if the substring "CHI" appeared anywhere in the full text, leading to an incorrect join and an undercounted total (243 instead of 3835).
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
In the final computation (function-call-17060121928014790421), the agent identified CHI papers by checking if the substring "CHI" appeared anywhere in the full text of each document loaded from paper_docs (function-call-7973633068397485439) and then summed 2020 citations from Citations (function-call-9866274534013825103), a flawed plan that misclassifies venue and yields 243 instead of the correct 3835.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: yes
FM3: no
FM4: no
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
In the final execute_python step, the agent naively extracted venue by regex-searching the full text for tokens like "CHI" and taking the first match before an inner join on title, causing many CHI papers to be misidentified or missed and resulting in an undercount (114 instead of 3835).
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@

A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent misidentified CHI papers by implementing venue extraction as a naive substring check for "CHI" anywhere in the full text (see execute_python), leading to an incorrect set of papers before summing 2020 citations and thus an undercount.

A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent extracted CHI venue by a naive, case-sensitive substring check ("CHI" in full text) on paper_docs before joining with 2020 citations and summing, which misidentified CHI papers and undercounted the total, leading to 243 instead of 3835.
